news.title: Samsung Confirms AI Smart Glasses Launch in 2026
news.summary: Samsung has announced plans to launch next-generation AI smart glasses in 2026, focusing on multimodal AI experiences combining voice, vision, and sensors for real-time interaction.
news.content: Samsung's EVP of Mobile Experiences, Seong Cho, confirmed the company's intention to enter the wearable AI market with smart glasses in 2026. These glasses will provide a 'multimodal AI experience', offering intuitive AI support for daily tasks and contextual assistance through voice commands and visual cues. The announcement came during Samsung's Q4 2025 earnings call, indicating a significant move into wearable technology.

news.title: Apple Plans to Launch AI Smart Glasses by Late 2026
news.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to release smart glasses with camera, microphone, and Siri integration in late 2026, aiming to compete with Meta and others in the wearables market.
news.content: According to Bloomberg, Apple plans to launch a pair of smart glasses equipped with a camera, microphone, and speaker in late 2026. These smart glasses will have the capability to execute voice commands through Siri and offer various functions such as analyzing the environment, making calls, playing music, providing real-time translation, and navigation services. This upcoming product from Apple is seen as a direct competitor to Meta Ray-Ban smart glasses.

news.title: Apple Testing Standalone Siri App for iOS 27
news.summary: Apple is reportedly testing a standalone Siri app for iOS 27 that works like a chatbot, with a new look and 'Ask Siri' button across apps.
news.content: In a move to enhance the AI capabilities of its mobile operating system, Apple is testing a standalone Siri app for iOS 27. The app is designed to function like a chatbot, offering a refreshed interface and an 'Ask Siri' button that will be accessible across various apps. This update signifies Apple's ongoing efforts to integrate AI more deeply into its ecosystem and improve user interaction with AI services.

news.title: Apple Watch and Apple Watch Ultra Models with Cameras Shelved
news.summary: Apple has decided to cancel the plans for camera-equipped Apple Watch and Apple Watch Ultra models that were expected in 2027.
news.content: While Apple is actively developing AI-powered smart glasses and AirPods with integrated cameras, the company has reportedly shelved plans for Apple Watch models with cameras. This decision comes as Apple focuses on other wearable technology advancements, such as smart glasses, and aims to maintain a balance between functionality and user privacy.

news.title: Nvidia Expects at Least a Trillion Dollars in Revenue Through Next Year
news.summary: Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, expects the company to generate at least a trillion dollars in revenue through next year, highlighting the growth of AI chip technology.
news.content: Jensen Huang, CEO of Nvidia, has expressed confidence in the company's financial performance, anticipating at least a trillion dollars in revenue for the coming year. This expectation is fueled by the increasing demand for AI chips, which are crucial for powering advanced AI applications and services. Nvidia's leadership in the AI chip market positions it well to capitalize on the growing AI technology sector.

news.title: Vivo Halts Smart Glasses Project
news.summary: Vivo has halted its smart glasses project after months of joint development with partners, including Goertek and ThunderSoft, due to a lack of differentiation in the market.
news.content: Vivo's decision to stop the development of its smart glasses project underscores the challenges faced by companies in the AI-powered eyewear market. The project was discontinued as executives concluded that the product did not offer enough differentiation in an already crowded market. This move reflects the broader difficulties in hardware complexity and the thin software ecosystem for smart glasses.

news.title: Google Releases Developer Preview 3 of Android XR SDK
news.summary: Google has released the third Developer Preview of the Android XR SDK, supporting early app creation for AI smart glasses.
news.content: As part of its broader Android XR initiative, Google has launched the third Developer Preview of the Android XR SDK. This move is aimed at enabling developers to start building applications tailored for AI smart glasses, fostering a strong software ecosystem prior to the commercial launch of these devices. Early partners like Uber and GetYourGuide are already testing augmented experiences, indicating the potential use cases for the technology in navigation, travel, and location-based services.

news.title: Google and Warby Parker Confirm 2026 Launch of AI Smart Glasses
news.summary: Google and Warby Parker have confirmed their partnership to launch AI smart glasses in 2026, signaling a new consumer push to integrate eyeglasses with generative AI.
news.content: The collaboration between Google and Warby Parker for the launch of AI smart glasses in 2026 highlights the growing interest in merging fashion and technology. This move is expected to bring AI-powered smart glasses to retail shelves, offering consumers a new way to access information and services hands-free. The launch is part of a broader industry shift towards wearable AI devices, with potential implications for how consumers interact with technology in their daily lives.

news.title: Nvidia Chief Eyes Space with AI Chip Technology
news.summary: Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, has stated that the company is heading for space with a goal of powering orbiting data centers using its AI chip technology.
news.content: Jensen Huang, CEO of Nvidia, announced the company's ambitious plan to expand into space by powering orbiting data centers with its advanced AI chip technology. This move signifies Nvidia's confidence in its ability to innovate and lead in the AI chip market, potentially revolutionizing data processing and storage in space.
